evals.name: semantic-drift-architecture
evals.prompt: I just finished refactoring the auth service at /Users/janno/Projects/claude-plugins-2/plugins/lunchbox/skills/council-workspace/test-projects/auth-service. The changes update the token structure to include expiration times and add a refresh token endpoint. I also added an AuthMiddleware class. Please run council on these changes.
evals.expected_output: The council should catch: (1) Semantic Alignment flags README still documents {userId, token} but code returns {id, accessToken, expiresAt}; (2) Architecture flags AuthMiddleware directly instantiates PostgresUserRepository instead of depending on an interface. Verdict: revise or rework, with specific file+location citations.

assertions.description: Output contains a Council Report header, confirming the skill was invoked and produced a structured report
Report flags the contradiction between README.md (userId/token) and code (id/accessToken/expiresAt)
Report flags that AuthMiddleware depends on a concrete class instead of an interface
Report cites specific file names or locations (src/auth.ts or src/middleware.ts)
Report references all six expert domains

evals.name: dry-clarity-violations
evals.prompt: Added the updateOrder function to the order processor at /Users/janno/Projects/claude-plugins-2/plugins/lunchbox/skills/council-workspace/test-projects/order-processor and created a processor utility module. Ready for council review.
evals.expected_output: The council should catch: (1) DRY flags updateOrder re-implements validateOrderInput logic inline instead of calling it; (2) Clarity flags handleData and processInfo as semantically empty function names. Specific lines and concrete fix suggestions expected.

assertions.description: Output contains a Council Report header, confirming the skill was invoked and produced a structured report
Report flags duplicated validation logic in updateOrder
Report flags handleData or processInfo as vague names
Report gives a concrete fix suggestion for at least one finding

evals.name: solid-semantic-multi-doc
evals.prompt: I extended PaymentService to handle email confirmations and audit logging directly in /Users/janno/Projects/claude-plugins-2/plugins/lunchbox/skills/council-workspace/test-projects/payment-gateway. The service now returns a PaymentResult object instead of void. Run council.
evals.expected_output: The council should catch: (1) SOLID S: PaymentService has 3 responsibilities; (2) SOLID D: EmailClient/fs are concrete dependencies; (3) Semantic: architecture.md says email belongs to NotificationService but code puts it in PaymentService; (4) Semantic: IPaymentGateway declares Promise<void> but impl returns Promise<PaymentResult>. Verdict: rework.

assertions.description: Output contains a Council Report header, confirming the skill was invoked and produced a structured report
Report flags that PaymentService now handles email, logging, and payment (S violation)
Report flags EmailClient or fs as concrete dependencies (D violation)
Report flags that architecture.md or README contradicts the implementation (NotificationService)
Report flags the Promise<void> vs Promise<PaymentResult> mismatch between interface and implementation
Both README and architecture.md are used as sources
